zh.Athelhampton, Dorset, England, United Ki: 英國英格蘭多瑟特阿瑟爾哈普頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Martin Baron of Athelhampton and Lord Mayor of London於1446年出生在英國多瑟特阿瑟爾哈普頓時，他的父親Sir Thomas Martin Baron of Athelhampton16歲，母親Elizabeth Clevedon21歲。他於大約1485年與Isolde Farington結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1504年3月24日在他的家鄉享年58歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭多瑟特普德萊托。

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Martin Baron of Athelhampton and Lord Mayor of London was born in 1446, in Athelhampton, Dorset, England, his father, Sir Thomas Martin Baron of Athelhampton, was 16 and his mother, Elizabeth Clevedon, was 21. He married Isolde Farington about 1485. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 24 March 1504, in his hometown, at the age of 58, and was buried in Puddletown, Dorset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Martin Baron of Athelhampton and Lord Mayor of London est né en 1446, à Athelhampton, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Martin Baron of Athelhampton, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Clevedon, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Isolde Farington environ 1485. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 24 mars 1504, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Puddletown, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat um 1485 Isolde Farington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 24. März 1504 im Alter von 58 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Puddletown, Dorset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Martin Baron of Athelhampton and Lord Mayor of London è nato il nel 1446 nel Athelhampton, Dorset, England, il suo padre, Sir Thomas Martin Baron of Athelhampton, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Clevedon, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Isolde Farington circa 1485. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 24 marzo 1504, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Puddletown, Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Martin Baron of Athelhampton and Lord Mayor of London(이)가 1446년 영국 도르세트 앳헬함프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Martin Baron of Athelhampton(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Clevedon(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1485년 Isolde Farington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1504년 3월 24일 그의 고향에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 푸들레타운에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Martin Baron of Athelhampton and Lord Mayor of London nasceu em 1446, em Athelhampton, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Martin Baron of Athelhampton, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Clevedon, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Isolde Farington aproximadamente 1485. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 24 de março de 1504, em sua cidade natal, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Puddletown, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Martin Baron of Athelhampton and Lord Mayor of London родился в 1446 г. в местности ателхамптон, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Martin Baron of Athelhampton было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Clevedon, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Isolde Farington около 1485 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 24 марта 1504 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности пуддлетоун, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Martin Baron of Athelhampton and Lord Mayor of London nació en 1446, en Athelhampton, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas Martin Baron of Athelhampton, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Clevedon, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Isolde Farington aproximadamente 1485. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 24 de marzo de 1504, en su pueblo, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Puddletown, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
